content.full_text:
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
(916) 322·5662 
Administration 
322·5660 
Executive/legal 
322·5901 
Enforcement 
322·6441 
Statements of Economic I nferest 
322-6444 
Mr. John Cook 
City Attorney 
531 K Street 
Eureka, CA 
95501 
Dear Mr. Cook: 
July 6, 1983 
Re: 
Advice Letter No. A-83-163 
This letter is to confirm my telephone advice to you 
concerning Eureka City Councilmember Thomas McMurray, Jr. 
facts that you presented to me during our conversation are 
The 
that 
the 
the City Council of the City of Eureka is going to vote on 
budgets for both the City and the Redevelopment Agency. 
Included in the budgets are appropriations for a water project 
which, if constructed, will affect an interest in real property 
owned by Councilman McMurray. 
You presented the following 
questions: 
A. 
If the budget decision concerning the water project is 
resolved separately from the remainder of the budget, and the 
councilmember disqualifies himself from this decision, may he 
participate in the other decisions concerning the budget, 
including the final, overall decision to approve or disapprove 
of the budget? 
B. 
If the budget decision on the water project is not 
separately considered, may the councilmember participate in the 
final, overall decision? 
Government Code Section 871001/ provides that no local 
public official shall make, or participate in the making of, a 
decision in which he knows or has reason to know he has a 
financial interest. 
An official has a "financial interest" in a 
decision within the meaning of Section 87100 if it is reasonably 
1/ Hereinafter all statutory references are to the 
Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 


Mr. John Cook 
July 6, 1983 
Page Two 
foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial 
effect on: 
* * * 
(b) 
Any real property interest in which the 
public official has a direct or indirect interest worth 
more than $1,000. 
For the purpose of our telephone conversation, we assumed 
that Councilmember MCMurray's real property interest would be 
materially affected by the construction of the water project. 
Thus, he must disqualify himself from considering those portions 
of the budgets that involve the appropriations for the water 
project. 
If, as you posed in your first question, the decision 
concerning the water project is resolved separately from the 
decisions concerning the remainder of the budget, the council-
member may participate in the other budget decisions and in the 
final, overall decision to approve or disapprove the budget. 
However, this requires that the decision regarding the water 
project be final and not subject to change by other budget 
decisions. 
If possible, this decision should be made before any 
other part of the budget is considered. 
In answer to your second question, if the decision on the 
water project is not separately considered, the councilmember 
may not participate in the final overall budget decision because 
he would continue to have a conflict of interest in the final 
decision. 
Thank you for your request for advice. 
If I can be of any 
help to you in the future, please feel free to contact me at 
916/322-5901. 
Very truly yours, 
Ja;u;s 51ian.k:J1{~~ 
Janis Shank McLean 
Counsel, Legal Division 
JSM: km

sections.conclusion: If the decision concerning the water project is resolved separately from the decisions concerning the remainder of the budget, the councilmember may participate in the other budget decisions and in the final, overall decision to approve or disapprove the budget, provided the water project decision is final and not subject to change by other budget decisions. If the decision on the water project is not separately considered, the councilmember may not participate in the final overall budget decision because he would continue to have a conflict of interest in the final decision.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a city councilmember with a financial interest in a water project included in a budget proposal may participate in other budget decisions or the final overall budget vote, depending on whether the water project decision is resolved separately from the remainder of the budget.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: A councilmember with a financial interest in a water project must disqualify himself from that specific decision. He may participate in other budget decisions and the final vote only if the water project decision is separately resolved and finalized first; otherwise, the conflict persists through the final budget vote.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: Clear advice letter format with defined question and conclusion sections. Document confirms telephone advice. Letter ID A-83-163. Written by Janis Shank McLean, Counsel, Legal Division.

QUESTION: A. 
If the budget decision concerning the water project is 
resolved separately from the remainder of the budget, and the 
councilmember disqualifies himself from this decision, may he 
participate in the other decisions concerning the budget, 
including the final, overall decision to approve or disapprove 
of the budget? 
B. 
If the budget decision on the water project is not 
separately considered, may the councilmember participate in the 
final, overall decision? 
Government Code Section 871001/ provides that no local 
public official shall make, or participate in the making of, a 
decision in which he knows or has reason to know he has a 
financial interest. 
An official has a "financial interest" in a 
decision within the meaning of Section 87100 if it is reasonably 
1/ Hereinafter Mr. John Cook 
July 6, 1983 
Page Two 
foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial 
effect on:

CONCLUSION: If the decision concerning the water project is resolved separately from the decisions concerning the remainder of the budget, the councilmember may participate in the other budget decisions and in the final, overall decision to approve or disapprove the budget, provided the water project decision is final and not subject to change by other budget decisions. If the decision on the water project is not separately considered, the councilmember may not participate in the final overall budget decision because he would continue to have a conflict of interest in the final decision.

embedding.summary: FPPC Counsel advises that Eureka City Councilmember McMurray must disqualify himself from budget decisions involving a water project that would affect his real property interests under Government Code Section 87100. Participation in other budget decisions and final approval is permitted only if the water project decision is separately and finally resolved before other budget matters are considered.
